description: OSHA recordkeeping, EPA requirements, refinery owner rules, union rules, arc flash

OSHA_reporting_timelines.how: Call OSHA area office, hotline 1-800-321-OSHA, or osha.gov
OSHA_reporting_timelines.clock_starts: When employer LEARNS of event, not when it happens

RECORDABLE: Foreign body removal from eye with surgery/magnet

common_refinery_owner_rules_beyond_OSHA: 100% tie-off at heights above 6 ft (retractable/double-leg lanyards)
Personal 4-gas monitor (LEL, O2, CO, H2S) for all in process areas
No cell phones in classified areas — need intrinsically safe devices
FR clothing required at all times — ALL layers must be FR
Short Service Employee program — green hard hat/sticker, paired with experienced worker
Stop Work Authority/Obligation — every worker expected to stop unsafe work
Pre-job JSA required before every task, every day — entire crew reviews and signs
Zero tolerance drugs/alcohol — random testing, positive = immediate removal

union_jurisdiction_examples.resolution: Threaded process connection = pipefitters. Direct wired = electricians. Calibration = instrument techs.
Electricians install inside tray. Ironworkers build support steel.
Pipefitter installs body in line. Instrument tech handles actuator/positioner/tubing. Electrician runs power/signal cable.

arc_flash_labels_must_show: Incident energy and working distance OR minimum arc rating OR PPE category
arc_flash_study_update: Must be updated when electrical system is modified (new transformer, changed breaker settings). Temp power also needs assessment.

EPA_relevance.transformer_oil: Counts toward SPCC plan threshold (>1320 gal aggregate). Large transformer can have 5000+ gal.
EPA_relevance.storm_water: NPDES permit required for sites disturbing 1+ acres. SWPPP document required.
